steps.title: Identify the punctuation relationships needed
steps.content: The blank follows the word music and must introduce the flute as the specific instrument being named — an appositive relationship. Then, after flute, the text shifts to a new descriptive idea (“relatively quiet instrument”) that works best as its own sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — music, the flute, a: Creates a comma-separated list that incorrectly lumps a predicate clause in with noun phrases, producing a run-on. ✗
  • Choice B — music. The flute, a: Ends the sentence at music, making The flute the subject of the next sentence with no appositive connector, leaving the sentence structure ambiguous. ✗
  • Choice C — music; the flute, a: A semicolon requires an independent clause on each side; what follows the flute is not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice D — music: the flute. A: The colon correctly introduces the flute as the named instrument; the period then starts a clean new sentence where A relatively quiet instrument is unambiguously the subject. ✓
D is correct.
